one_line_takeaway: 2S-GDA improves black-box transfer attacks on vision-language retrieval models by dropping SGA's destabilizing third (re-perturbation) stage and replacing greedy top-1 token substitution with a globally-diverse candidate pool plus loss-maximizing selection, yielding up to 11.17-point ASR gains over SGA-BSR on Flickr30K and 1.57-7.51-point gains when composed into existing DRA/SA-AET frameworks.
key_finding: On the full 4x4 source-target ASR grid (Table 1), 2S-GDA achieves ~100% ASR in white-box (diagonal) settings and outperforms all four baselines in black-box (off-diagonal) transfer settings, with the paper reporting overall gains of 0.11% to 11.17% ASR over baselines. Concrete examples from Table 1: ALBEF-to-CLIPViT TR R@1 ASR of 74.85% for 2S-GDA vs. 63.68% for the next-best baseline SGA-BSR (an 11.17-point gap matching the headline claim); ALBEF-to-CLIPCNN IR R@1 of 81.34% vs. 73.34% for SGA-BSR. When composed with existing frameworks (Table 2, ALBEF-generated examples), 2S-GDA-DRA and 2S-GDA-SA-AET outperform DRA-BSR and SA-AET-BSR respectively by 1.57 to 7.51 points in black-box settings (e.g., ALBEF-to-CLIPViT TR R@1: 2S-GDA-SA-AET 80.98% vs. SA-AET-BSR 75.21%). Ablation (Table 3, on ALBEF-to-CLIPViT transfer via three backbones) shows removing the two-stage restructuring (w/o 2S) causes the largest ASR drop of the three components, confirming the paper's claim that eliminating the unstable text re-perturbation stage is the dominant driver of the improvement over CTE or GAR alone. A separate feature-space analysis (PDD of Euclidean distance and cosine similarity between original and adversarial embeddings) is used to justify the two-stage redesign: the discarded third stage (S3, text re-perturbation) produces adversarial examples closer to the original input (Euclidean distance 14.82 vs. 17.64 for S2, cosine similarity 0.49 vs. 0.43 for S2), i.e., weaker perturbations, motivating its removal.
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution is 2S-GDA (Two-Stage Globally-Diverse Attack), a black-box transferable adversarial attack against vision-language pre-training (VLP) retrieval models. It restructures the prior 3-stage SGA pipeline (text -> image -> text) into a 2-stage pipeline (text -> image only), removing the final text re-perturbation step which the authors show (via feature-space PDD analysis) reduces perturbation strength rather than helping it. Within the text stage, the authors introduce a 'Globally-Diverse Strategy' with two components: (1) Candidate Text Expansion (CTE), which builds a substitution candidate pool by combining BERT-MLM top-k predictions with WordNet synonyms (rather than only the top-1 MLM token used by prior greedy methods), and (2) Globally-Aware Replacement (GAR), which selects the token-candidate pair maximizing adversarial loss (Eq. 4) among the top-k gradient-saliency-identified important tokens, rather than substituting the top-1 token greedily. The image stage adopts multi-scale resizing and BSR (block shuffle and rotation, itself a prior technique from Wang et al. 2024, cited as [11]) to build a diverse augmented image set for optimizing the visual perturbation. 2S-GDA is presented as modular: it can be composed with existing attack frameworks (DRA, SA-AET) as a drop-in replacement for their text-perturbation stage.
threat_model: Black-box transfer attack on image-text retrieval. An attacker crafts a joint adversarial example (v', t') from a paired image-caption input using white-box access to a surrogate VLP model (ALBEF, TCL, CLIPViT, or CLIPCNN), then evaluates transfer to a different, unseen target VLP model to which the attacker has no gradient access. Perturbation budgets are constrained: image perturbations bounded by PGD with l-infinity epsilon_v = 8/255 (step size 2/255, T=10 iterations), and text perturbations bounded by BERT-Attack with epsilon_t = 1 substituted token, candidate list size W = 10, k = 3 influential tokens considered. The attack goal is to misalign the fused multimodal embedding so that Rank-1 image-text or text-image retrieval fails (measured as Attack Success Rate, ASR, on Rank-1 accuracy).
limitations: The paper's own stated scope limits: results in the extracted main text are reported only on Flickr30K; MSCOCO results are deferred to a supplementary material not present in the extracted pages, so this review cannot verify cross-dataset generalization numbers. Comparisons to older methods (PGD, BERT-Attack, Sep-Attack, Co-Attack) are also deferred to supplementary material and not verifiable from the paper. The paper does not evaluate against any adversarial defense or detection mechanism -- it is purely an attack-effectiveness study with no discussion of mitigations, robustness training, or detectability of the generated adversarial text/image perturbations. The ablation in Fig. 5 shows ASR peaking at k=3 influential tokens and declining beyond that, attributed only to 'excessive semantic distortion' without a deeper mechanistic explanation. The threat model assumes the attacker has white-box (gradient) access to at least one surrogate VLP model, which the paper does not consider a limitation but is a fixed precondition for the whole line of work (consistent with cited prior work, not the paper's own novel scoping choice).

datasets_used: Flickr30K (1000 images, 5 captions each) -- primary, results present in extracted text
MSCOCO -- mentioned as evaluated in supplementary material, not present in extracted text
quantitative_results: Table 1: ALBEF(source)->CLIPViT(target) TR R@1 ASR: 2S-GDA 74.85% vs SGA-BSR 63.68% vs SGA 39.14% vs DRA 47.12% vs SA-AET 55.58%
Table 1: ALBEF->CLIPCNN IR R@1 ASR: 2S-GDA 81.34% vs SGA-BSR 73.34%
Overall black-box ASR improvement over baselines stated as 0.11% to 11.17%
Table 2: ALBEF-generated 2S-GDA-SA-AET vs SA-AET-BSR, CLIPViT TR R@1: 80.98% vs 75.21%
Table 2: overall improvement from combining 2S-GDA with DRA/SA-AET stated as 1.57% to 7.51% higher ASR
Feature-space PDD analysis of SGA stages S2 vs S3: Euclidean distance 14.82 (S3) vs 17.64 (S2); cosine similarity 0.49 (S3) vs 0.43 (S2)
Table 3 ablation (ALBEF->CLIPViT-derived, SA-AET-BSR backbone): Completed 2S-GDA TR R@1 80.98%/IR R@1 84.89% vs w/o 2S 80.25%/84.66% vs w/o CTE 77.67%/81.12% vs w/o GAR 79.63%/81.86%
Attack hyperparameters: image PGD epsilon_v=8/255, step 2/255, T=10 iterations; text BERT-Attack epsilon_t=1, candidate list W=10, k=3 influential tokens (ASR peaks near k=3 per Fig. 5)
baselines_compared: PGD, BERT-Attack, Sep-Attack, Co-Attack (mentioned as reported only in supplementary material, not in extracted main text)
